title: Message Envelopes, Binary Bodies, Schemas, and Evolution · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers message envelopes, binary bodies, schemas, and evolution into a new versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects.
Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them.
Validate unknown envelopes and payloads with strict runtime schemas, bounded collections, semantic refinements, and explicit rejection evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Buffer Decoding Boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 9736 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Buffer Decoding Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Buffer Decoding Boundary, keep Buffer Decoding Boundary connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 4688 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use asyncapi 3.1 and cloudevents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, keep Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.
